concept: And (correlative closer) / Completing the both-and pair
description: The closing member of the correlative pair 'lera...lero' (both...and). Announces that what follows is the second and final element of an emphatic additive pairing. Completes the structure opened by 'lera', confirming that both elements together form a deliberate whole.
sound_symbolism: Shares the liquid 'l' onset with 'lera', binding the pair acoustically. The shift from 'a' to 'o' in the final vowel signals completion and closure, the rounding of 'o' wrapping around the open 'a' that began the construction. The listener hears the phonetic kinship and knows the pair is resolved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as the second member of the correlative conjunction pair 'lera...lero'. Appears before the second coordinated element. Must always be paired with a preceding 'lera'. The complete construction is: 'lera [element 1] lero [element 2]'.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The closing correlative confirms the balanced, non-hierarchical pairing announced by the opener. Both elements are grammatically and structurally equal, embodying the principle that connection need not imply subordination.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The satisfying resolution of the paired structure creates a sense of completeness, echoing the oral tradition of balanced, memorable phrasing.
